new_classes.label_en: Other wine (neither PDO/PGI nor Tokaj) in containers ≤ 2 L (subheading 2204 21 78)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Wein (weder g.U./g.g.A. noch Tokaj) in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L (Unterposition 2204 21 78)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes in containers of 2 litres or less that is neither a wine with a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) or Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), nor a Tokaj wine, nor a semi-sparkling wine; classified under CN subheading 2204 21 78. The total dry matter content (non-volatile substances including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matters and salts) is a key classification criterion per Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22.

Quality of a wine expressing the sum of its non-volatile substances (total dry matter / Gesamttrockenstoff) including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matters, and salts, as defined by Additional Note 4A to Chapter 22 of the CN.

coverage_explanation: The axioms cover the core distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 21 78: (1) wine of fresh grapes in small containers (≤ 2 L) via the SmallContainer parthood restriction; (2) exclusion of PDO/PGI wines (2204 21 68, 2204 21 74), Tokaj wine (2204 21 23), and semi-sparkling wines (2204 21 06–09) via complement restrictions; (3) introduction of a TotalDryMatterContent quality class referencing Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22. Coverage is not 1.0 because: (a) the precise quantitative thresholds defined by Additional Note 4A (grams per litre of total dry matter distinguishing sweet, medium-sweet, etc.) cannot be fully captured without introducing numeric facet restrictions on the new quality, which would require additional datatype properties scoped to TotalDryMatterContent that are not yet in the TBox; (b) the specific list of non-volatile substances (sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matters, salts) is documented in the definition but not fully formalised as part restrictions; (c) Additional Note 5 criteria (sweetness categories) are not yet represented as named quality individuals in the TBox.
